_comment: Microsoft 365 PowerShell Module Configuration - ISO 27001 Evidence Collection + dev profiles
_instructions: Set 'enabled': false to disable a service area or individual module. Profiles select which services install.
_changes: (1) ALL Microsoft.Graph.* unified to 'latest' to stop assembly-load version skew. (2) Added 'devworkstation' profile. (3) Added Az.ContainerRegistry; trimmed rarely-used Az.* to enabled:false. (4) Enabled Microsoft.Graph.Applications. (5) Disabled archived MSAL.PS. (6) Added PSReadLine/Posh-Git for dev shells. NON-PowerShell workstation setup (winget/VSCode/env) lives in the separate workstation-setup repo, NOT here.
_graphVersionNote: Microsoft.Graph.* sub-modules MUST all be the same version or you get 'Assembly with same name is already loaded'. Keep them ALL 'latest' OR ALL pinned to one identical version. Never mix.

Microsoft.Graph.Authentication.description: Microsoft Graph authentication - required for all Graph operations
Microsoft.Graph.Authentication._note: CHANGED 2.0.0 -> latest to match sibling Graph modules

MSAL.PS.description: Microsoft Authentication Library for advanced auth scenarios
MSAL.PS._note: DISABLED: MSAL.PS is archived/unmaintained by Microsoft. Prefer Microsoft.Graph.Authentication or the MSAL.NET assembly shipped with Az.Accounts. Re-enable only if a legacy script imports it.

Microsoft.Graph.Identity.Governance.description: Identity governance: access reviews, entitlement management, PIM

Microsoft.Graph.Applications.description: App registrations, service principals, enterprise apps
Microsoft.Graph.Applications._note: ENABLED: app-registration / service-principal management is core to managing the agent app regs.

ExchangeOnlineManagement.description: Exchange Online administration (mailboxes, mail flow, compliance, IPPS)

Microsoft.Graph.Teams.description: Teams via Graph API (compliance, advanced management)

sharepoint.name: SharePoint Online
sharepoint.description: SharePoint sites, lists, and content management

PnP.PowerShell.description: Modern SharePoint administration (recommended). NOTE: 2.x requires PS7 and its own Entra app registration for app-only auth.

Microsoft.Online.SharePoint.PowerShell.description: Classic SharePoint Online management (legacy, Windows PowerShell only)

Microsoft.Graph.DeviceManagement.description: Intune device management, compliance policies, configuration profiles

security.name: Security & Compliance
security.description: Security alerts, policies, and compliance features

Microsoft.Graph.Security.description: Security alerts, incidents, and threat intelligence

Microsoft.Graph.Identity.SignIns.description: Sign-in logs, conditional access, risk events

reporting.name: Reporting & Analytics
reporting.description: Usage reports, analytics, and document generation

ImportExcel.description: Excel file creation and manipulation (no Excel install needed)

powerplatform.name: Power Platform
powerplatform.description: Power Apps, Power Automate, Power BI administration

Microsoft.PowerApps.Administration.Power.description: Power Apps tenant and environment administration

Microsoft.PowerApps.PowerShell.description: Power Apps development and management tools

azure.name: Azure Integration
azure.description: Azure services that integrate with Microsoft 365 / GMS agents

Az.Storage.description: Azure Storage (blobs, files, queues, tables, Terraform state)

Az.KeyVault.description: Azure Key Vault for secrets and certificate management (BTG auth)

Az.ContainerRegistry.description: ACR push/pull/import for the container-app release flow
Az.ContainerRegistry._note: ADDED: present on the current workstation, used by the ops release/ACR workflows.

development.name: Development & Automation
development.description: Tools for PowerShell development, testing and CI/CD

PSScriptAnalyzer.description: PowerShell script quality analysis and best practices

Pester.description: PowerShell testing framework (v5.x required - NOT compatible with the v3.4.0 shipped with Windows PowerShell)
Pester._note: The Windows-shipped Pester 3.4.0 lives in AllUsers scope and CANNOT be removed from CurrentUser scope; import the explicit version: Import-Module Pester -RequiredVersion 5.7.1

PSReadLine.description: Command-line editing, history and prediction for the PS7 console

Posh-Git.description: Git integration for PowerShell (prompt + tab completion)

basic.name: Basic Administrator
basic.description: Essential modules for day-to-day Microsoft 365 administration

security.name: Security Administrator
security.description: Core modules plus security and compliance tools

iso27001.name: ISO 27001 Evidence Collection
iso27001.description: All modules required for ISO 27001:2022 Annex A.8.x evidence collection

devworkstation.name: GMS Developer Workstation (PowerShell layer only)
devworkstation.description: PowerShell modules for managing the GMS GHE repos: Graph + Exchange/IPPS + Azure (KV/ACR/Storage/Monitor) + dev/test tooling. The non-PowerShell layer (winget tools, VS Code, git/gh/env, Claude config) is handled by the separate workstation-setup repo, which calls this script with -Profile devworkstation.
devworkstation._comment: Lean profile. Does NOT install Intune/Teams/SharePoint/Power Platform by default - add them to this array if a given repo needs them.

developer.name: Developer/Automation
developer.description: Core modules plus development and automation tools

enterprise.name: Enterprise Administrator
enterprise.description: All modules for comprehensive Microsoft 365 and Azure management
